message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step reasoning by main node, incorporating cross-references:

1) REG-010 (Art9(8)): The node requires testing of high-risk AI systems throughout development and, in any event, pre-market/service; testing must use prior defined metrics and probabilistic thresholds appropriate to intended purpose. The policy chunk requires “pre-release” evaluation results to be documented and sets up ongoing evaluation cadence. It does not negate testing throughout development nor the requirement to use predefined metrics/thresholds; it is silent on those specifics. Under the omission rule, silence is a Non-Deviation. No contradictions or narrowed scope are evident.

2) REG-039 (Art15(2)) with cross-reference REG-038 (Art15(1)): Art15(2) assigns the Commission the role to encourage development of benchmarks/methodologies to measure the accuracy/robustness required by Art15(1). The policy chunk speaks to the company’s internal evaluation methods/documentation and transparency notes; it neither asserts anything about the Commission’s role nor conflicts with Art15(1)’s required outcomes. Absence of any statement concerning the Commission is an omission and must be treated as Non-Deviation. No contradiction or narrowing is present.

3) REG-027 (Art13(3)): The node (as provided) requires instructions for use to include the provider’s identity/contact and performance characteristics, capabilities, and limitations including intended purpose. The policy chunk commits to providing customer documentation describing intended uses and evidence of fitness for each intended use, and when a platform service, including this in a Transparency Note. This does not contradict the requirement to include specified information in “instructions for use”; it is silent on identity/contact details and on the exact artifact named “instructions for use.” Per the omission rule, this is Non-Deviation; there is no explicit statement replacing or negating instructions for use or excluding required elements.

Conclusion: No explicit deviations are identifiable from the provided policy chunk for any of the three nodes.
</deliberation>
